content.text: Concave Mirror: Converging mirror (curves inward). Convex Mirror: Diverging mirror (curves outward).
Pole (P): Center of mirror surface. Focus (F): Point where parallel rays converge/diverge. Focal Length (f): Distance P to F ($f = R/2$).

content.name: Mirror Equation
content.note: u = object distance, v = image distance, f = focal length. Use New Cartesian Sign Convention.

content.name: Magnification
content.note: m > 1: Magnified; m < 1: Diminished; m is negative: Real/Inverted; m is positive: Virtual/Erect.

content.label: 💡 Pro Tip:
content.text: Convex mirrors ALWAYS form virtual, erect, and diminished images, making them perfect for rear-view mirrors in vehicles.
